content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as inherently "curiosity-partitioned" architectures, where long-term memory is dynamically segmented based on how much intrinsic reward a curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agent assigns to each encountered interaction. This creates a self-organizing memory hierarchy that prioritizes high-uncertainty, learnable experiences over redundant or unsurprising ones without manual memory pruning.
